figures.caption: Fig. 4: Qualitative comparison under camera movement. (a) Veo 3.1 [6], (b) CogVideoX [8], (c) Tora [7], and (d) Ours. Each column corresponds to the camera viewpoint indicated by the colored camera icons.
Fig. 8: Additional qualitative results on curated scenes.

Fig. 7: Integration of environmental dynamics with localized object-centric motion. Environmental dynamics (e.g., water flow) coexist with motion applied to selected regions such as flags and tree branches.
Fig. 5: Qualitative comparison of motion consistency across different methods on the same input image. (a) Naive Scene Flow, (b) WonderWorld [5] + 3D-MOM, and (c) Ours.
Fig. 9: Additional qualitative results on in-the-wild scenes.

Fig. 2: Overall framework of the proposed method. Starting from the previous 4D scene, the environment is progressively expanded through camera movement and outpainting. Motion cues estimated from user-guided masks are aligned using a geometry-aware alignment module and integrated into a hash-based motion field, enabling bidirectional motion propagation for temporally coherent 4D scenes.
